On Polymarket, the live trading of odds is probably most effective with stuff like fighting

Fighting is different than football and baseball because when you get hit once or get put in a submission hold, the odds can change pretty quick

It makes a ton of sense why you would use something like Polymarket for trading UFC markets

Since launching Breakout our goal has been to constantly iterate in the RIGHT direction in an industry where many are moving backwards. 

More assets, new terminal, more evaluation types and of course better rules! 

Our max drawdown is static, it does not move. 

Ready to try? 

Use code MAYNE at checkout for a discount.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
A "static" drawdown that jumps up to your starting balance after your first payout isn't static.

It moved. That's not very static of you, guys.

Ours is set once when you buy the account.
Win, lose, withdraw, the floor doesn't move.

thread_tweets.full_text:
Since launching Breakout our goal has been to constantly iterate in the RIGHT direction in an industry where many are moving backwards. 

More assets, new terminal, more evaluation types and of course better rules! 

Our max drawdown is static, it does not move. 

Ready to try? 

Use code MAYNE at checkout for a discount.

thread_tweets.text:
The 3% is the total you can lose in a 24h window, based on your closed balance, and it resets daily. 

So if you're down 1.5-2%, you stop, wait for the reset, and you can trade again with a fresh 3% daily DD. 

What you're describing is full-porting your whole account at 5x into one position. 

The daily drawdown exists to discourage exactly that. We'll let you do it, it's just not the optimal way to trade prop.

Most people using prop firms get this. They risk 0.5-1% per trade because they don't need to full-port max leverage. The whole point of prop is you're already working with a bigger account than you'd have on your own.

And to give you a comp outside of prop. A $1,000 account at 100x gets liqd on a 1% move.

A $100k account at Breakout costs $330-$800 depending on the eval. That fee is your entire max downside.

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

US successfully escorted one group of tankers in through the Oman lane.

All of them had their AIS off, but there was one Indian cargo ship that had it on when it went through. The list can be seen below.

Following two days of US strikes, the US is back to escorting ships in. My guess is that it will try to escort a group out tonight, and Iran can either respond and create more escalation going into the weekend or it can lose leverage.

The Oman lane will be heavily contested in the coming days, which leaves either side with no choice but to escalate.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.
tweets.full_text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

US successfully escorted one group of tankers in through the Oman lane.

All of them had their AIS off, but there was one Indian cargo ship that had it on when it went through. The list can be seen below.

Following two days of US strikes, the US is back to escorting ships in. My guess is that it will try to escort a group out tonight, and Iran can either respond and create more escalation going into the weekend or it can lose leverage.

The Oman lane will be heavily contested in the coming days, which leaves either side with no choice but to escalate.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.

tweets.text: @notthreadguy We used to be able to be vulgar

thread_tweets.text: @notthreadguy We used to be able to be vulgar
thread_tweets.full_text: @notthreadguy We used to be able to be vulgar

thread_tweets.text:
Not certain but some things that come to mind

- cancel culture / pussification of society
— downside of being cancelled outweighs upside of making the joke, can barely lean into stereotypes without people losing their minds 

- broadcast monoculture has been dead since iPhone adoption  
— harder to make common knowledge jokes based on relevant pop culture (this is why you have to explain nine layers of a joke now)

tweets.text: According to @LawrenceLepard, it's a fact that Bitcoin has gone up for 17 years. But the fact is, Bitcoin is lower today than it was in April 2021. That's over five years ago. Versus gold, Bitcoin is down 58% since its peak in Nov. 2021. It's Lepard who doesn't know the facts.

tweets.text: Bitcoin was never really correlated to gold, despite the false claims that it’s digital gold. However, there was a strong correlation to the NASDAQ that has since broken down. Bitcoin no longer rises when the NASDAQ rises. But, I think it will still fall when the NASDAQ falls.

tweets.text:
My other takeaway from the 2040ai version a path, is that it says Americans will have ubi at much higher rates than the rest of the planet ($1 mil per U.S. adult) and then giving something like $15k to some allies in other countries.

So in this scenario means that:
1. US citizens will be top 5% globally
2. Illegal immigration will become an even bigger hot topic bc the chasm between the haves (Americans) and have nots (non Americans ex China) will start to become so wide with such high stakes.  Need to get our house in order faster.
